Threat: Unauthorised public viewing of dashboard data
Vulnerability: Web security group allows HTTP from 0.0.0.0/0 and the dashboard displays operational/personnel data
Event / Incident: Anyone with the public IP can view the dashboard
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: Public HTTP is explicitly configured and the app renders data in the page.

Threat: Personnel data disclosure through the dashboard
Vulnerability: Application renders personnel_data fields and seed data contains names, contacts, roles and clearance levels
Event / Incident: Employee information is shown to an unauthorised viewer
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8 Asset management / A.9 Access control / A.18 Compliance
Likelihood Justification: The sensitive fields are directly present in the seed file and display code.

Vulnerability: PHP display_errors is enabled and database connection exceptions are printed to users
Event / Incident: Application failure exposes technical information in the browser
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: The CloudFormation UserData explicitly enables error display and prints exception messages.

Vulnerability: PHP app connects to MongoDB using the Mongo EC2 public IP and a mongodb:// URI
Event / Incident: The application reaches the database through public addressing rather than a private controlled path
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.10 Cryptography / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: The public IP and plain MongoDB URI are visible in the application setup.

Threat: Unauthorised public viewing of dashboard data
Vulnerability: No authentication gate is shown before the dashboard renders data
Event / Incident: Unauthenticated visitor reaches the dashboard page
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / OWASP Broken Access Control
Likelihood Justification: The generated PHP page renders data directly and no login check is visible in the reviewed code.

Vulnerability: CloudFormation template defines HTTP port 80 and no HTTPS or TLS termination evidence is shown in reviewed files
Event / Incident: Users access the dashboard over unencrypted HTTP
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.10 Cryptography / A.13 Network security
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 205-215; no HTTPS listener certificate or TLS termination resource found in reviewed CloudFormation template
Likelihood Justification: OWASP lists cryptographic failures as a major web risk; live AWS console should confirm whether any external TLS layer exists.

Threat: Software supply chain weakness during bootstrapping
Vulnerability: Composer installer and PECL package installation run during instance boot without checksum verification shown
Event / Incident: Instance build depends on external package downloads during boot
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because the risk depends on package/download compromise or failure, but the boot-time dependency is visible.

Threat: Unauthorised modification of web files after instance access
Vulnerability: Web root ownership and permissions are changed recursively to apache:apache and 755
Event / Incident: An attacker with process-level access may alter dashboard files more easily
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.12 Operations security
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because it depends on instance compromise, but the permission change is visible in code.

Vulnerability: Application source is generated inside EC2 UserData startup script rather than stored as a reviewed application file
Event / Incident: Dashboard code changes are harder to review and test separately from infrastructure changes
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because this is a maintainability and review risk in the deployment design.

Vulnerability: MongoDB find() is used without limit, pagination, projection or filtering on displayed collections
Event / Incident: Dashboard may render all available records and selected sensitive fields
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because current seed data is small, but the code pattern would expose more records if added.

Vulnerability: Timestamp rendering assumes valid timestamp objects while E_ALL and display_errors are enabled
Risk Source: Data quality issue / external attacker if data can be influenced
Event / Incident: Malformed or missing timestamp values may expose warnings or break the page
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium-low because current seed timestamps are valid, but the error display configuration makes failures visible.

Threat: External attacker connects directly to database
Vulnerability: MongoDB security group allows 27017 from 0.0.0.0/0 and MongoDB binds to 0.0.0.0
Event / Incident: Internet host attempts direct MongoDB connection
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security / ISO 27017 cloud network controls
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 154-164, 193-195; MongoDB SG screenshot needed
Likelihood Justification: The database port is internet-open and MongoDB listens on all interfaces.

Vulnerability: MongoDB EC2 is deployed in PublicSubnet1 and the stack outputs the database public IP
Event / Incident: Database becomes discoverable and reachable as a public cloud asset
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / ISO 27017 shared responsibility
Likelihood Justification: Public subnet placement and public IP output are concrete deployment properties.

Vulnerability: MongoDB user creation and security.authorization are not shown in the reviewed UserData script
Event / Incident: Database service may not require application-specific authenticated access until configured
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / MongoDB Security Checklist
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 179-195; MongoDB auth check needed
Likelihood Justification: MongoDB's security checklist recommends access control and authentication; the reviewed UserData startup script does not show this setup.

Vulnerability: MongoDB TLS configuration is not shown for server or client connection
Event / Incident: Database traffic may be sent without transport encryption
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.10 Cryptography / MongoDB TLS guidance
Likelihood Justification: MongoDB recommends TLS for incoming and outgoing connections; the reviewed files do not show TLS options.

Vulnerability: No EBS snapshot, AWS Backup, or restore process is defined in the CloudFormation baseline
Event / Incident: Database data is lost and no recovery evidence is available from the CloudFormation baseline
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.17 Continuity / A.12 Operations security
Evidence / Notes: No backup resource is defined in cfstack.yml; AWS Backup screenshot needed
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because absence is from reviewed baseline files only; impact is high because data is central to the app.

Threat: Database software supply chain or install failure
Vulnerability: MongoDB repository and GPG key are added during instance boot from external URLs
Event / Incident: Database build depends on external repository availability and trust during boot
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because the dependency is visible, but compromise or outage is not proven.

Vulnerability: MongoDB is deployed as one EC2 instance with no replica set or failover resource shown
Event / Incident: Database instance failure stops the dashboard from reading data
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.17 Continuity / A.12 Operations security
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 170-195; no replica set resource shown
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because single-instance deployment is visible, but actual uptime depends on AWS and usage.

Threat: Unsupported or unverified database patch state
Vulnerability: MongoDB is installed at boot but no post-deployment patch verification process is shown
Event / Incident: Database package may become outdated without a documented review process
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.6 Organization of information security
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 183-195; patch status screenshot needed
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because install is visible but live patch state requires AWS verification.

Threat: Data exfiltration from compromised database host
Vulnerability: MongoSecurityGroup defines inbound rules but no restrictive egress rules are shown
Event / Incident: Stolen database data could leave the host through default outbound access
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / A.12 Operations security
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 154-164; no restrictive outbound rule evidence found for Mongo SG
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because exploitation first needs compromise, but egress restriction is not defined in reviewed baseline files.

Threat: Exposure of database data at rest
Vulnerability: No explicit encrypted EBS volume, KMS key or BlockDeviceMappings are shown for the MongoDB instance
Event / Incident: Stored database files may not have evidenced encryption at rest
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.10 Cryptography / A.8 Asset management
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 170-195; EBS encryption screenshot needed
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because account-level EBS encryption may exist, but it is not evidenced in the reviewed CloudFormation template.

Vulnerability: No mongodump, snapshot, AWS Backup, retention or restore procedure is shown in reviewed files
Event / Incident: The team cannot prove recovery after data loss or instance failure
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.17 Continuity / A.12 Operations security
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 170-195; backup and restore evidence needed
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because absence is from reviewed baseline files only, but recovery failure is credible for a self-hosted database.

Vulnerability: Database seed contains personnel records and operational logs
Event / Incident: A database breach exposes employee and operational information
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8 Asset classification / A.9 Access control / A.18 Compliance
Likelihood Justification: Sensitive data exists in the database and other rows show public exposure paths.

Vulnerability: The dashboard displays operational logs and event details from system_operational_logs
Event / Incident: Operational events and subsystem details are viewed through the public dashboard
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8 Asset management / A.9 Access control
Likelihood Justification: The dashboard display code and seed data show operational log details available to the application.

Threat: Exposure of city sensor and transport data
Vulnerability: Seed data includes traffic flow, air quality and transport service alert records
Event / Incident: City operations data is viewed or copied outside the intended team
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8 Asset management / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because sensitivity is lower than personnel data but exposure paths exist.

Vulnerability: No classification or handling label is shown for personnel or operational data in the repo
Event / Incident: Team may treat sensitive and non-sensitive records the same way
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8 Asset classification
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because the data exists but classification process is not shown in reviewed files.

Vulnerability: No schema validation, required field checks or collection validators are shown before inserting or displaying records
Risk Source: Bad data / unauthorised write after DB exposure
Event / Incident: Malformed records may affect dashboard output or decision making
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because malformed data requires write access or bad seed data, but exposed DB design increases concern.

Vulnerability: No unique indexes are shown for record_id employee_id or log_id
Event / Incident: Duplicate logical records may reduce trust in dashboard data
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium-low because current seed data is small, but repeated inserts can create inconsistent records.

Threat: Weak separation between public and private tiers
Vulnerability: Private subnets exist but MongoDB and webserver are both placed in PublicSubnet1
Event / Incident: Internal database service is not separated from the public tier
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / ISO 27017 cloud network controls
Likelihood Justification: The CloudFormation template creates private subnets but does not place MongoDB there.

Vulnerability: Public subnets assign public IPs and public route table sends 0.0.0.0/0 to the internet gateway
Event / Incident: Resources in the public subnet can become reachable from the internet
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: AWS public subnet behaviour is defined by public IP assignment and route to internet gateway.

Threat: Limited network visibility during attack or misuse
Vulnerability: VPC Flow Logs are not defined in the reviewed CloudFormation baseline
Event / Incident: Suspicious traffic is harder to investigate after an incident
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Logging and monitoring / A.16 Incident management
Evidence / Notes: No VPC Flow Logs resource is defined in cfstack.yml; AWS console check needed
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because it is an evidence gap in the CloudFormation baseline, not proof that logging is absent in AWS.

Vulnerability: CloudWatch alarms for webserver or MongoDB are not defined in the reviewed CloudFormation template
Event / Incident: Service issues or attack symptoms may not alert the team quickly
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Logging and monitoring / A.16 Incident management
Evidence / Notes: No CloudWatch alarm resource is defined in cfstack.yml; AWS console check needed
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because this is based on reviewed baseline files and requires AWS console confirmation.

Threat: Over permissive database security group source
Vulnerability: MongoDB security group uses 0.0.0.0/0 instead of the webserver security group as source
Event / Incident: Database accepts traffic attempts from any internet address instead of only the application tier
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is high because the public source is explicitly configured in the database security group.

Threat: No controlled administrative access pattern shown
Vulnerability: CloudFormation template uses an EC2 key pair parameter but does not show SSM Session Manager or bastion pattern
Event / Incident: Instance management may rely on direct key-based access outside a controlled access process
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because key pair use is visible but actual admin practice needs confirmation.

Vulnerability: The CloudFormation baseline does not define custom network ACL rules for public and private subnet boundaries
Event / Incident: Subnet-level filtering is not documented in the reviewed baseline files
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security
Evidence / Notes: No custom Network ACL resource is defined in cfstack.yml
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because absence is from reviewed baseline files and must be checked in AWS.

Vulnerability: Public subnets auto-assign public IPs so later EC2 resources placed there become public by default
Event / Incident: Future resources inherit public exposure by subnet placement mistake
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / AWS SEC05
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is high-medium because MapPublicIpOnLaunch is explicitly enabled on both public subnets.

Vulnerability: Security groups define inbound rules but no restrictive SecurityGroupEgress rules are shown
Event / Incident: Compromised web database or VDI host can make outbound connections without CloudFormation-level egress restriction
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / AWS SEC05
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 154-164, 205-215, 391-401; no restrictive outbound rule evidence found
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is high-medium because egress restriction is not defined in reviewed baseline files, though AWS console must confirm final rules.

Threat: No second subnet-level network filter shown
Vulnerability: No custom Network ACL resources are defined for public and private subnet boundaries
Event / Incident: Subnet boundary filtering relies mainly on security groups in the reviewed CloudFormation template
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / AWS SEC05
Evidence / Notes: cfstack.yml lines 49-95; no AWS::EC2::NetworkAcl resource is defined
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because this is based on reviewed CloudFormation absence and should be checked in AWS.

Threat: Failed secure migration to private subnets
Vulnerability: Private subnets exist but no NAT Gateway or VPC endpoints are defined for private workload package installs
Event / Incident: Moving workloads private may break boot-time package installation without additional egress design
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / A.12 Operations security
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium-low because this affects the future secure design, not the current public deployment only.

Threat: Insecure CloudFormation baseline remains after setup
Vulnerability: CloudFormation baseline environment contains open security group rules and public service exposure
Event / Incident: The same insecure baseline can remain if the group only documents it and does not treat it later
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: The insecure settings are visible in the baseline and should be treated later rather than just described.

Vulnerability: DBLoad.js drops all three collections before inserting seed data
Event / Incident: Script is run against an environment containing data that should be retained
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.17 Continuity
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is lower because it depends on script execution, but the destructive action is in code.

Threat: Controls not recorded clearly after changes
Vulnerability: Security changes made in AWS console may be hard to prove if screenshots and notes are not kept
Event / Incident: The team cannot clearly show what changed and why
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.14 Secure development
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because this is a process risk unless evidence is collected consistently.

Threat: Limited deployment audit trail in coursework evidence
Vulnerability: Screenshots and command outputs are not yet attached to the repo evidence folder
Event / Incident: The group cannot prove what was deployed or checked during assessment
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.18 Compliance
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because this is a current evidence gap, not a technical exploit.

Vulnerability: Linux and Windows AMI IDs are hard-coded instead of using a patch-tracked parameter or Launch Template
Event / Incident: Instances may start from images whose patch status has not been evidenced
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8.8 Vulnerability management / A.8.9 Configuration management
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because AMI patch status must be verified in AWS.

Vulnerability: No separate data volume, snapshot protection or rebuild recovery evidence is shown for MongoDB
Event / Incident: Rebuilding the environment may remove database data if recovery is not prepared
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8.13 Information backup / A.8.32 Change management
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because data persistence and backup controls need AWS verification.

Threat: Remote desktop attack surface if GroupSize is 3
Vulnerability: Optional RDP security group allows 3389 from 0.0.0.0/0 and VDI is in PublicSubnet1
Initial Treatment Direction: Avoidance if not used / Control if used
Event / Incident: Public internet hosts can attempt remote desktop access if VDI is deployed
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: The risk only applies when the optional VDI condition is used.

Vulnerability: GroupSize 3 silently creates VDI with public RDP exposure controlled by a group-size parameter
Initial Treatment Direction: Avoidance if not used / Control if used
Event / Incident: A group size change creates a security-sensitive public remote access host
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.5.8 Project management / A.13 Network security
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because it depends on GroupSize 3, but the conditional deployment is explicit.

Vulnerability: Windows VDI has public RDP but no visible UserData startup script hardening SSM path IAM role logging or patch baseline
Initial Treatment Direction: Avoidance if not used / Control if used
Event / Incident: Public remote host is deployed without hardening evidence in the reviewed CloudFormation template
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.8.8 Vulnerability management / A.8.16 Monitoring activities
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is high-medium if VDI is used because public RDP is visible and hardening is not shown in reviewed baseline files.

Vulnerability: VDI webserver and MongoDB are all placed in PublicSubnet1
Initial Treatment Direction: Avoidance if not used / Control if used
Event / Incident: Compromised VDI is close to the web and database tier in the same subnet
ISO 27001 / ISO 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / AWS SEC05
Likelihood Justification: Likelihood is medium because compromise is a prior condition, but shared subnet placement is explicit.
